accurate.why: All claims match the bill text; the description of report and review requirements is accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: States 'GAO' as the reviewing entity, but the bill text specifies the Comptroller General—the head of GAO—alongside FEMA. While GAO is technically correct as the Comptroller General's agency, this is a minor but acceptable characterization. More precisely, the bill says the Comptroller General, not 'GAO' as an institution.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose as requiring FEMA and GAO to study and report on disability disaster assistance.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The bill does not amend existing law, and the summary correctly notes this.

effective_dates.why: Correctly identifies 120-day and one-year deadlines with approximate calendar dates.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers both the FEMA report and the broader review, including the physical disabilities expansion.

correct_entities.why: FEMA Administrator, Comptroller General, and relevant committees are correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: 120-day and one-year deadlines are accurate; approximate calendar dates derived from the November 2024 enactment are reasonable.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
